title: galium angustifolium subsp. gracillimum
intro: Galium angustifolium subsp. gracillimum, also known as Mountain Avens, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and fighting off infections. Galium angustifolium subsp. gracillimum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and salves, which are commonly used in traditional medicine.
